name: Sunday Loaf
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 18
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease a 9x5x3-inch loaf pan. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Stir with a fork to mix all dry ingredients well. Cut the butter into small pieces and add it to the flour mixture. Using your fingers or a pastry cutter, rub or cut the cold butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Beat the eggs well in a small bowl, then add the jam or preserves and milk; whisk until the mixture is blended smoothly. Stir the egg mixture into the flour mixture and stir only until no flour streaks remain—the mixture will be lumpy. Spoon the batter into the loaf pan and bake for about 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until a straw inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow the loaf to cool in the pan for 15 to 20 minutes before turning it out onto a rack. Meanwhile, make the glaze by combining butter, sugar, and jam in a pan. Stir over low heat until the butter is melted and the mixture is smooth. Strain if necessary and spoon the glaze over the loaf after it has cooled on the rack.
